mechanism_narrative: RSAFS (retail sales) in levels serves as a proxy for consumer demand strength. Higher retail sales directly boost corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This signal is always active regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 20-day forward returns weighted by RSAFS level below 0.03 for any 2-year rolling window, or negative correlation significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, it signals that monetary policy is restrictive. Elevated policy rates compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and increase recession risk, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return after trigger is not negative at 5% significance over rolling windows of 3 years, or if the signal produces positive returns in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When both UNRATE is below 4% and PAYEMS is above 156 million, the labor market is extremely tight. This condition forces wages up, erodes profit margins, and eventually triggers monetary tightening, all of which are negative for equities over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition holds, or the signal's rank IC over the past 5 years is below 0.03 for horizon_hint=60.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment (CPIAUCSL > 300), further increases in industrial production (INDPRO) are interpreted as demand-pull inflation that will force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, thus harming equity values over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when CPIAUCSL > 300, the correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative (i.e., not significantly negative at 10% level), or the rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,500 thousand units indicate significant weakness in the housing sector, which is a leading indicator of economic recession. Such weakness precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate earnings decline.
failure_condition: If after trigger, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive or not significantly different from zero at 5% level over the last 15 years, or if the frequency of positive returns exceeds 60%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) and low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) defines a late-cycle overheating regime. In this regime, further economic strength is likely to accelerate policy tightening, leading to a sharp downward repricing of equities over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when trigger is active over the last 10 years, or the signal's rank IC < -0.03 (expected) is not achieved.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) levels reflect the overall supply-side capacity of the economy. Higher industrial output indicates robust economic activity and corporate profitability, which supports higher S&P 500 prices over 20 trading days. This is a persistent trend signal active in all regimes.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO level and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-positive over any 5-year rolling window, or rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A total nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) above 156 million indicates an extremely strong labor market. Such tightness signals robust economic growth and consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings higher, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after trigger, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is negative or zero over the last 5 years, or if the hit rate of positive returns is below 50%.
